title: evodianthus funifer subsp. funifer
intro: Evodianthus funifer subsp. funifer is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, where it is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and fever. The plant is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting relaxation. Evodianthus funifer subsp. funifer can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some cases, its extracts are used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
